patches.hypothesis: The original patch was overfitting the test input by using hardcoded values and not handling general cases. The refined patch fixes the root cause by using more general and robust logic.
patches.original_hypothesis: the bug is caused by the method not handling the case where the input value is not Comparable.

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by the method throwing an IllegalArgumentException instead of a ClassCastException when it encounters a non-comparable value. The test expects a ClassCastException to be thrown.
patches.changes:
- throw new IllegalArgumentException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
+ throw new ClassCastException();
patches.diff:
- throw new IllegalArgumentException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
+ throw new ClassCastException();

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by the method not handling the case where the input object is not comparable to existing values in the freqTable. The fix involves updating the method to handle this case by throwing a ClassCastException instead of an IllegalArgumentException.
patches.changes: + throw new ClassCastException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
patches.diff: + throw new ClassCastException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
